news.headline: Moderna and Walgreens saw a drop in their shares last week, with Walgreens falling by 24.12%. Other companies affected include Floor & Decor Holdings, which lost 13.41% in reaction to economic data.
news.summary: The top ten large-cap stocks witnessed a decline in the past week, with noticeable drops in their share prices. Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ:WBA) saw a significant decrease of 24.12% following the release of its Q3 earnings report, coupled with a downward revision of its 2024 guidance that fell below market expectations. This resulted in analysts revising their price targets for the stock. Floor & Decor Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:FND) also experienced a drop of 13.41%, which could be attributed to the disappointing May Pending Home Sales economic data. The shares of Moderna, Inc. (NASDAQ:MRNA) also fell during this period. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

Global easing measures continue despite Fed's delay in rate cut. Economists expect significant impact.
Wild Forest sold $1M in NFTs in under a minute, transitioning from web2 to web3 for innovation on Ronin network.
news.summary: Wild Forest, a popular web3 real-time strategy game, has made a significant impact in the gaming world. Lead developer Artem Naseniuk shared insights on the game's evolution from web2 to web3. Naseniuk stated that the team's inspiration came from their love for strategy games like "Warcraft 3," leading to the inception of Wild Forest in 2013. The shift to web3 was motivated by the team's desire for innovation and dissatisfaction with the stagnant web2 gaming market. Choosing the Ronin network for its advanced technology and supportive community, Naseniuk praised its technical sophistication and community engagement. This transition to web3 has brought Wild Forest success and recognition in the gaming industry.

news.headline: Economist Paul Krugman believes inflation is beaten as annual PCEI hits 2.6%, lowest since March 2021, increasing chances of rate cuts.
news.summary: Renowned economist and Noble Prize winner Paul Krugman made remarks about the recent inflation data, indicating a decrease in pricing pressure and raising the likelihood of rate cuts. Krugman stated that "inflation looks beaten," following the Bureau of Economic Analysis’ report showing the personal consumption expenditure index (PCEI) annual rate at 2.6%, the lowest since March 2021. This metric is significant as it is the Fed’s preferred inflation measure. Krugman also posted a chart illustrating the annual rates of PCEI and core PCEI, attributing the previous blip to start-of-year price adjustments and linking the remaining pressure to delayed housing costs.

news.headline: Top gainers last week include Rivian Automotive (+30.04%), Carnival Plc (+17.78%), and FedEx Corporation (+17.51%).
news.summary: The top performers in the last week among large-cap stocks were Rivian Automotive, Inc. (NASDAQ: RIVN) with a 30.04% surge driven by reports of output simplification, cost reduction, and a joint venture with Volkswagen. Carnival Plc (NYSE: CUK) saw a 17.78% increase following strong Q2 earnings. FedEx Corporation (NYSE: FDX) experienced a 17.51% stock surge due to impressive Q4 financial results and guidance. These stocks led the market and displayed significant growth, making them potential considerations for portfolio inclusion. For more details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Yesterday, it was reported that the total number of confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the country has reached 100,000, with 5,000 new cases reported in the past 24 hours. They are also urging the public to practice social distancing and good hygiene to help prevent further transmission of the virus.

news.headline: Martina Cheung is set to become the new President and CEO of S&P Global, succeeding Douglas Peterson. Cheung, with 14 years at the company, has been leading S&P Global Ratings, which reported $3.3 billion in revenue in 2023, up by 9% from the previous year. She will assume the role on November 1, 2024, and join the Board on July 1, 2024. Peterson will remain on the Board until May 2025 and be a special advisor until December 31, 2025.
news.summary: S&P Global Inc. announced Martina L. Cheung as the new President and CEO, succeeding Douglas L. Peterson, effective from November 1, 2024. Cheung, the current President of S&P Global Ratings, will also join the Board starting July 1, 2024. Peterson will continue on the Board until May 2025 and will act as a special advisor until December 31, 2025. Cheung, who has been part of S&P Global for 14 years, oversaw S&P Global Ratings reporting revenue of $3.3 billion in 2023, a 9% increase from the previous year, along with a 56.5% operating profit margin. Additionally, under her guidance, S&P Global Sustainable saw a notable 24% revenue growth in 2023. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Aramco awards $25bn in contracts for gas expansion with phase two at $12.4bn and phase three at $8.8bn. 23 rig contracts at $2.4bn.
news.summary: The second phase of the ambitious Jafurah gas development has recently kicked off with the award of contracts totaling approximately $12.4 billion. In addition, contracts worth about $8.8 billion have been granted for the expansion of the Company's Master Gas System in phase three. Furthermore, the project includes the signing of 23 additional contracts for unconventional gas rigs, amounting to $2.4 billion. Additionally, two directional drilling contracts have also been awarded as part of the development.

news.headline: Mauritania's President Ghazouani likely avoids runoff with 56% of votes.
news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that a record-breaking number of unemployment claims have been filed in the country, reaching over 3.5 million in a single week. This surge in jobless claims is believed to be a result of the ongoing global pandemic, with many businesses forced to close their doors or reduce operations. The unprecedented increase in unemployment numbers has put a strain on the economy and has led to concerns about the long-term effects on the labor market. Economists are closely monitoring the situation and are calling for government intervention to support those who have lost their jobs during this challenging time.

news.headline: Dow Jones drops post housing data, Rivian surges 30% with $5B Volkswagen investment, challenging Tesla.
news.summary: Today, the stock market experienced a decline as the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped following the release of significant housing data. One notable standout was Rivian, a Tesla competitor, which saw a remarkable increase of 30% in its stock price after receiving a substantial $5 billion investment from Volkswagen. This news resulted in a surge of interest and confidence in Rivian's growth potential.

news.headline: U.S. manufacturing rebounding, orders for capital goods rise, durable goods orders show steady growth. Production up by 0.9% in May. Inflation easing, CPI rose 3.3%.
news.summary: The U.S. manufacturing sector is showing signs of recovery after recent struggles due to inflation and high borrowing costs. In May, orders for U.S. manufactured capital goods rose 0.1%, surpassing the expected decline of 0.1%. Durable goods orders increased by 0.1% in May, following a 0.2% gain in April. Production at U.S. factories grew by 0.9% in May, after a 0.4% decline in April. Year over year, production at U.S. factories rose by 0.1% in May. Inflation, a significant threat to the sector, remained steady in May with a 3.3% rise year over year, slightly lower than expected. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell noted the easing of inflation.

news.headline: High post-debate uncertainty for investors. Fed's PCE inflation gauge: Headline PCE 0.0%, Core PCE 0.1%. Personal spending drops while personal income rises. Investors should separate political views from investing. Arora Report suggests waiting for post-debate clarity. Winners could be Magnificent Seven, mega cap, and fossil fuel energy stocks.
news.summary: The stock market is showing signs of attempting to break to a new high with technical setups indicating a potential increase. However, the stock market is currently significantly above the support zone and has low volume, signaling a lack of conviction among investors. The latest PCE data revealed that both headline and core PCE matched the consensus expectations at 0.0% and 0.1% respectively. In terms of consumer-based economic indicators, personal spending was lower than expected at 0.2% while personal income exceeded expectations at 0.5%. Political uncertainty following the presidential debate has impacted the American economy and international standing. Prudent investors are advised to wait for clarity post-debate, with potential changes to portfolios based on ensuing developments. The Arora Report suggests that the Magnificent Seven stocks, along with other mega-cap and fossil fuel energy stocks, could be winners after the debate.

FDA approves AbbVie/Genmab's Epkinly for refractory follicular lymphoma, targeting 15,000 patients yearly. Commercialization shared, further global effort by AbbVie.
news.summary: The FDA recently granted approval for Epkinly (epcoritamab-bysp) as the first T-cell-engaging bispecific antibody that can be administered subcutaneously to adults who are suffering from relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma (r/r F) after undergoing at least two prior lines of therapy. This groundbreaking treatment is a result of a collaborative effort between AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV) and Genmab A/S (NASDAQ: GMAB) in the field of oncology. Notably, the FDA's Accelerated Approval program granted this indication based on the promising overall response rate (ORR) and durability of response.Moving forward, both companies will be sharing commercial responsibilities in the U.S. and Japan, with AbbVie leading global commercialization efforts. However, the approval for this indication from the FDA could be subject to further confirmation of clinical benefits through additional trials. It is essential to note that approximately 15,000 individuals are diagnosed with follicular lymphoma in the U.S. every year, and current standard-of-care therapies have limited efficacy due to the disease being incurable.This approval by the FDA was made based on the results obtained from Phase (unspecified) of the clinical trials. More details regarding this significant development can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Whirlpool shares dip as Bosch bid rumors fade. Analyst sets $118 price target, noting Whirlpool's strong market position.
news.summary: Whirlpool Corporation (NYSE:WHR) stocks are down today after a surge yesterday following reports of a potential bid from Robert Bosch GmbH. Goldman Sachs analyst Susan Maklari labeled the stock as Buy with a price target of $118. Maklari noted that Bosch holds a 6% share in the global appliance market but only 1.5% in North America, compared to Whirlpool's leading 25% market share in North America and 10% globally in 2023. Details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Stocks show modest gains ahead of Fed's inflation report, with chipmakers struggling. Gold and Bitcoin rebound. US banks pass 2024 stress tests. Regional banks outperform larger counterparts. Semiconductor sector faces challenges. Treasury yields lower.
news.summary: Wall Street had a quiet trading day as investors awaited the Federal Reserve's inflation data release. U.S. banks cleared the 2024 stress tests, proving they can meet capital requirements in severe economic conditions. Regional banks, tracked by KRE, performed better than larger banks. Major indices saw slight gains with small caps leading. The semiconductor sector struggled, with SOXX down 0.7% and Nvidia dropping almost 2%. Treasury yields fell as investors sought bonds before the inflation report. The 10-year Treasury bond yield decreased by 4.

news.headline: Schlumberger secures contract with Equinor for 12-well electric Subsea Production Systems project in Norway; future contracts dependent on final investment decision. Stocks of both companies rise.
news.summary: Shares of Schlumberger Limited (SLB) and Equinor ASA (EQNR) are up today following Schlumberger's successful bid for a contract from Equinor. The contract pertains to the front-end engineering design of a 12-well, all-electric Subsea Production Systems (SPS) project in the Fram Sør field offshore Norway. As part of the agreement, SLB OneSubsea will handle future engineering, procurement, and construction contracts upon a final investment decision. This project is slated to utilize SLB OneSubsea's expertise.

news.headline: UnitedHealth's OptumRx settles opioid prescription claims for $20M after improperly filling certain prescriptions, first settlement with a PBM, DEA investigation revealed irregularities in filling opioid prescriptions in California from 2013-2015.
news.summary: OptumRx, a pharmacy benefit manager under UnitedHealth Group, has reportedly settled for $20 million in response to U.S. government claims of improperly filling certain opioid prescriptions. This marks the first agreement between the government and a PBM concerning illicit opioid prescriptions. The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) investigated OptumRx and found that a California mail-order pharmacy filled opioid prescriptions alongside drugs like benzodiazepines and muscle relaxants from 2013 to 2015. Additionally, OptumRx's new Transparent Drug Pricing Initiative for 2024 has been introduced. The full story is available elsewhere.

news.headline: On Thursday, CDC's ACIP recommended Merck's Capvaxive for adults 65+ for pneumonia. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices has endorsed Merck & Co Inc's Capvaxive (Pneumococcal 21-valent Conjugate Vaccine) for adults aged 65 and above. Also known as the pneumonia vaccine, the committee unanimously recommended a single dose of Capvaxive for individuals:- Aged 65 and older who have not received a pneumococcal conjugate vaccine before or have an unknown vaccination history. - Aged 19-64 with specific underlying medical conditions or other risk factors who have not previously been vaccinated.

news.headline: Regeneron Pharma (NASDAQ:REGN) gets positive opinion for odronextamab in Europe for R/R FL/DLBCL. European Commission expected to approve.
news.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals Inc (NASDAQ:REGN) recently revealed that the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has recommended conditional approval of odronextamab for relapsed/refractory (R/R) follicular lymphoma (FL) or R/R diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) in cases where two or more lines of systemic therapy have been tried with positive outcomes. R/R follicular lymphoma (FL) denotes FL recurring after initial treatment or failing to respond to it, while R/R diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) indicates a similar situation with DLBCL. The European Commission is anticipated to approve the recommendation soon.

news.headline: Google and TikTok ban telehealth firm's ads for ADHD medication following federal crackdown on Done Global. DOJ charges 5 individuals in a conspiracy by Done to illegally distribute Adderall.
news.summary: Google and TikTok have implemented bans on Done Global from advertising on their platforms due to a federal crackdown on the telehealth company's distribution of Adderall and other stimulants. Adderall is widely used for treating ADHD. The Justice Department has pressed charges against five additional individuals linked to an alleged conspiracy at Done to unlawfully distribute Adderall and other controlled substances. This development comes after investigations by The Wall Street Journal and almost two years after Done forfeited a necessary third-party certification. For more details, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: On April 9, 2024, APA Corporation (Nasdaq: APA) revealed adjustments to its executive leadership team to align with a restructuring of its development function and the integration of the acquired Callon Petroleum. These changes are now in effect.

news.headline: Kanen Wealth criticizes Fossil for slow restructuring, cites capital incompetence, requests board seat.
news.summary: Kanen Wealth Management has pointed out Fossil's capital allocation ineffectiveness and has requested a position on the board to address this issue. They have raised concerns regarding Fossil's handling of funds, advocating for better financial decisions to be made by the company. Kanen Wealth Management's emphasis on Fossil's improper allocation of capital underscores the need for strategic changes to improve the company's financial performance.

news.headline: Athabasca Minerals Inc. approved to delist from TSXV following reorganization, pending acquisition by Badger Mining Corp.
news.summary: Athabasca Minerals Inc. announced on April 19, 2024, that they have applied to delist their common shares (TSXV: AMI) from the TSX Venture Exchange. This decision is part of a larger agreement with Badger Mining Corporation made on February 9, 2024, in which Badger will acquire all of the Common Shares. The Transaction has been approved by an order of the Court of King’s Bench of Alberta under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, following an application on the morning of April 19, 2024.

news.headline: Smart for Life completes restructuring program reducing material losses and transforming balance sheet. Company updates on Form 10-K.
news.summary: A recent program by the company has led to significant improvements in operations by reducing material losses and transforming the balance sheet. This initiative has resulted in notable benefits for the company, as reflected in the Form 10-K update. The program's success is evidenced by the reduction in losses and the positive impact on the company's financial statements. The company has also provided an update on the expected timeline for the Form 10-K submission, highlighting the positive outcomes achieved through the operational improvements.
